analysis.problem: Groundwater depletion and contamination are threatening drinking water supplies and ecosystem health in multiple regions, including Florida and Michigan.
analysis.root_cause: Excessive groundwater pumping, drought, and industrial contamination are depleting aquifers and introducing harmful substances into water sources.
leverage_points: Regulatory enforcement of groundwater extraction limits
Investment in water treatment and pollution prevention technologies

proposed_actions.action: Implement stricter regulations on groundwater extraction in aquifer-dependent regions
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce groundwater extraction rates by 20% within five years

proposed_actions.action: Fund large-scale water treatment and desalination projects in affected regions
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve 30% improvement in water quality in contaminated areas within ten years

evidence: Florida’s Floridan Aquifer is being overpumped, leading to sinkholes and declining water quality
Michigan’s aquifers are under stress due to drought and contamination from heavy metals
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the broader domain of planetary health, as groundwater depletion affects both human populations and ecosystem stability, with cascading effects on food security, biodiversity, and climate resilience.
analysis.summary: Groundwater depletion and contamination are causing severe water shortages and health risks, requiring urgent regulatory and technological interventions.

raw_items.title: Drought continues to impact water quality and river levels across...
raw_items.summary: The recent drought has taken a toll on groundwater levels and water quality across Florida. Despite this, drinking water supply for residents and water supply used to extinguish recent wildfires has had little to no impact.

raw_items.title: What’s Causing Sinkholes to Multiply in Florida?
raw_items.summary: Florida’s primary water source, the Floridan Aquifer, is one of the most productive aquifer systems in the world, supplying drinking water to millions of residents. However, excessive groundwater pumping is directly linked to the increased formation of sinkholes across the state.

raw_items.title: Florida is building the world's largest environmental restoration project
raw_items.summary: The ecosystem recharges the water supply for all of South Florida – a population approaching nine million. South Florida's primary source of drinking water comes from groundwater stored in aquifers, mainly the Biscayne Aquifer, which is replenished by the Everglades.
